catalysts.catalyst_details: Modest potential risk to software sector estimates due to tariffs.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We remain positive on software, especially given recent pullback, while acknowledging modest potential risk to estimates from tariffs.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Potential risk to current CY25 guidance for software companies.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We explore two key themes: 1) Potential risk to current guidance (CY25)

catalysts.catalyst_details: Possibility of an 'air-pocket' or softening demand in the software sector as the year unfolds.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: At best, Management teams will hold guidance unchanged near-term, although it’s possible we might enter an air-pocket as the year unfolds as we previously highlighted here.

catalysts.catalyst_details: CY25 average revenue growth for the software sector could see a 100-300 bps reduction.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: This implies CY25 average revenue growth (GSe +13%) could see a 100–300 bps reduction (albeit -4% drawdown in USD since C1Q close vs. major currencies could be a partial mitigant).

catalysts.catalyst_details: A re-rating of the software sector to a normalized multiple (~8x EV/Sales NTM) could unlock meaningful upside into CY26.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: At ~6x EV/Sales NTM, downside is modest if CY25 estimates are revised lower, while a re-rating to ~8x (normalized multiple) could unlock meaningful upside into CY26.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Management teams are well-equipped to preserve FCF through cost agility, with Gen-AI investments unaffected.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Management teams remain well-equipped to preserve FCF through cost agility, with Gen-AI investments unaffected.

catalysts.catalyst_details: AI is seen as the software sector’s key catalyst, especially as focus moves from infrastructure/training CapEx to inferencing use cases at platform and application layers.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We see AI as the sector’s key catalyst, especially as focus moves from infra/training CapEx to inferencing use cases at the platform and application layers.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Leading growth indicators (cRPO, NER, new logos) stabilized in the second half of 2024 after a multi-year deceleration.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe the worst is behind us for software fundamentals with leading growth indicators (cRPO, NER, new logos) stabilizing in C2H24 after a multi-year deceleration from peak levels.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Announcement of new tariffs on April 2nd, leading to uncertainty and potential impact on software purchasing behavior.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Following the April 2nd tariff announcement, we are following up on our initial Tariff Note to provide a range of Revenue/FCF scenarios (Exhibit 2) for our broader coverage to help investors better assess the potential knock-on effects to broader software purchasing behavior relative to the operating assumptions implicit in Management guidance exiting C4Q24.

catalysts.catalyst_details: C1Q results for the software sector are expected to be largely insulated from potential demand fallout associated with the tariff announcement, though April-close C1Q's may see modest impact.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Note that at a sector-level, we believe C1Q results will largely be insulated from any potential demand fallout associated with the tariff announcement (albeit April-close C1Q’s may observe a modest degree of impact on Revenue and cRPO)

catalysts.catalyst_details: Modest risk that management teams may provide more conservative guidance for C2Q and CY25 compared to Street expectations, due to tariff uncertainty.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: but we acknowledge the modest risk potential that Management teams may opt to provide more conservative guidance on C2Q (vs. Street and implied guidance) and CY25.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Management teams are likely to report deal cycle elongation, reduced contract sizes, and/or broader freezes in software procurement entering the June quarter (April/May).
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: For subscription and consumption models alike, Management teams are likely to call out some level of deal cycle elongation, reduced contract sizes and/or broader freezes in software procurement entering the June quarter (April/May); at a minimum, they’re likely to acknowledge the risk of such a dynamic impacting upcoming quarters.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Revenue growth could be impacted by -70 to -280 bps assuming -5% to -20% cuts to net new revenue (NNR) due to tariff uncertainty.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our net new revenue sensitivity analysis implies that average revenue growth for our coverage in CY25 (GSe: 13%) could come down -70 bps in a more conservative scenario of a -5% cut to NNR and upwards of -280 bps in a downside scenario where NNR is cut by -20% (Exhibit 2).

catalysts.catalyst_details: FCF growth can remain resilient even if a revenue slowdown scenario transpires, due to potential for FCFM expansion.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Revenue growth could be impacted by -70 to -280 bps assuming -5% to -20% cut to current NNR, albeit FCF growth can remain resilient if our slowdown scenario transpires.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The software sector is structurally more profitable as companies right-sized their cost structures following a post-COVID growth slowdown (CY22-24).
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: One key reason is that the sector is structurally more proﬁtable as companies right-sized their cost structures following a post-COVID growth slowdown (CY22-24).

catalysts.catalyst_details: Analysis of net new revenue trends suggests potential for modest cuts if trends softened post-tariff announcement.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our analysis of net new revenue trends pre- and post-guidance points suggests potential for modest cuts if trends softened post-tariff announcement.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Management teams may incrementally de-risk expectations for 2Q and CY25 during the upcoming March/April quarter reporting season to factor for short-term demand fallout from tariff uncertainty.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As such, we acknowledge the modest risk potential that Management teams incrementally de-risk expectations for 2Q and CY25 during the upcoming March/April quarter reporting season to better factor for any short-term demand fallout from tariff uncertainty.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Tariff uncertainty may cause management teams to revisit their assumptions around Net Expansion Rate (NER) and new logo acquisition if there is demand degradation in April.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: However, we don’t rule out the possibility that the impact of tariffs may cause Management teams to revisit their assumptions around NER and new logo acquisition if there were to be any demand degradation in the month of April.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Leading indicators for revenue growth were beginning to firm up in C2H24, with NER stabilizing and sequential customer growth inflecting modestly.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Leading indicators for revenue growth were beginning to ﬁrm up in C2H24, with NER stabilizing in the low-110’s (Exhibit 7) and sequential customer growth inﬂecting modestly to +3% (from a +2% trough in C1H24, Exhibit 6) across our coverage.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The knock-on effect of tariffs could cause enterprises to temporarily pause hiring plans, delay purchasing decisions, and pull back on transformational projects until tariff policies crystallize.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: On net, we anticipate that the knock-on effect of tariffs could cause many enterprises to temporarily pause hiring plans (impacting software seat growth), delay ongoing purchasing decisions, and/or generally pull back on more transformational projects until tariff policies crystallize.

catalysts.catalyst_details: cRPO growth rate and revenue coverage ratio will be important gauges of investors’ confidence in underwriting CY25 expectations.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: cRPO growth rate and revenue coverage ratio will be an important gauge of investors’ conﬁdence in underwriting CY25 expectations.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Software companies are entering CY25 with a slightly higher proportion of committed revenue (68% vs. 67% in C1Q24) relative to prior years, which could limit negative guidance revisions.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: While the environment has clearly changed, we note that companies in our coverage are entering CY25 with a slightly higher proportion of committed revenue vs. NTM guidance relative to the prior years (68% vs. 67% in C1Q24), which could help put a ceiling on the magnitude of potential negative guidance revisions (if any at all).

catalysts.catalyst_details: Software companies have variable cost leverage that can likely blunt the impact of any potential revenue growth slowdown, by adjusting operating costs.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Variable cost leverage can likely blunt the impact of any potential revenue growth slowdown.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Software companies are better equipped to navigate macro uncertainty due to structurally improved profitability from significant cost reduction initiatives post-COVID.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe software companies are better equipped to navigate through a potential growth slowdown relative to prior cycles considering most companies undertook signiﬁcant cost reduction initiatives post-COVID that translated to structurally improved margins, namely though workforce reductions, vendor consolidation and broader IT optimization.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Gen-AI is poised to drive the next leg of software growth despite short-term tariff-induced volatility.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Gen-AI is poised to drive the next leg of software growth despite short-term tariff-induced volatility.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Tariff disruptions, even if indirect, have the potential to accelerate AI adoption as enterprises seek productivity gains.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Tariff disruptions, even if indirect, have the potential to accelerate adoption as enterprises seek productivity gains.

catalysts.catalyst_details: 2025 may not be a major inflection point for Gen-AI due to budgetary constraints, but software companies are expected to foster engagement and adoption of productivity-driving AI products.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Albeit, this year may not mark a meaningful inﬂection point for Gen-Al given potential budgetary constraints against a more volatile macro backdrop, but we do expect many software companies to foster engagement and adoption of products that can drive real productivity against across the enterprise.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Stability in the macro environment and pervasive AI across workflows can pave the way for upside in software growth expectations in CY26.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As such, if we see stability in the macro environment and AI becomes more pervasive across workﬂows, we believe this can pave the way for upside for software growth expectations in CY26.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The emergence of a Gen-AI 'killer application' with meaningful utilization and monetization could become a catalyst for the software sector.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As such, we note that the market might be slow to participate in the Gen-AI theme until early signals of an ‘killer application’ with meaningful utilization and monetization emerges, which we then believe can become a catalyst for the sector.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Laying the groundwork in fostering AI adoption in 2025 is setting the foundation for a more robust value creation cycle and growth upside in 2026.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We acknowledge that while 2025 may not mark the breakout year for Gen AI or the emergence of a “killer app, ” we believe it is setting the foundation for a more robust value creation cycle in 2026.

catalysts.company: ServiceNow Inc., Salesforce Inc., Adobe Inc.
catalysts.catalyst_details: User conferences in late-Q2/Q3 (ServiceNow Knowledge, Salesforce Dreamforce, Adobe Max) could be a turning point for the Gen-AI narrative, providing greater visibility on AI adoption and revenue contribution.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As pricing models shift and feature sets mature throughout 2025, we believe user conferences in late-Q2/Q3 (such as ServiceNow Knowledge, Salesforce Dreamforce, Adobe Max) could be a turning point in the Gen-AI narrative, where we expect to gain greater visibility around AI adoption and revenue contribution.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The product innovation cycle in 2025 can pave the way to AI-driven value creation in 2026.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Product innovation cycle in 2025 can pave the way to AI-driven value creation in 2026.

catalysts.catalyst_details: AI-driven value creation will more meaningfully permeate in 2026 with a more contained inflationary environment, improved economic growth, and stability from tariff-induced volatility.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe AI-driven value creation will more meaningfully permeate in 2026 with a potentially more contained inﬂationary environment, improvement in economic growth, and stability from tariff-induced volatility.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Adobe is generating over $125M of AI-specific ARR and influencing more than $3.5B of AI-enabled bookings.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Tangible proof points already include Adobe generating over $125M of Al-speciﬁc ARR and inﬂuencing more than $3.5B of Al-enabled bookings;

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow saw Pro Plus deals more than quadruple year-over-year, including 39 deals with 3+ Now Assist products.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: ServiceNow saw Pro Plus deals more than quadruple yoy, including 39 deals with 3+ Now Assist products;

catalysts.catalyst_details: Salesforce is counting 3,000 paid AgentForce customers and roughly $900M of combined Data Cloud and AI ARR.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Salesforce counting 3,000 paid AgentForce customers and roughly $900M of combined Data Cloud and Al ARR;

catalysts.catalyst_details: Intuit is reporting a 20% higher ecosystem attach rate for AI-powered QuickBooks Live users.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Intuit reporting a 20% higher ecosystem attach rate for Al-powered QuickBooks Live users;

catalysts.catalyst_details: Microsoft is disclosing a $13B annualized run rate from AI services.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: and Microsoft disclosing a $13B annualized run rate from AI services;

catalysts.catalyst_details: Datadog is attributing 6% of both year-over-year growth (CY24) and total ARR to AI-native clients.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: and Datadog attributing 6% of both yoy growth (CY24) and total ARR to Al-native clients.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Twilio reported $260M in revenue from customers building in the AI space at its latest Investor Day.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Twilio also reported $260M in revenue from customers building in the AI space at its latest Investor Day.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Intrinsic Value Analysis indicates that most of the software coverage universe now trades at a discount.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We reiterate our Intrinsic Value Analysis, which indicates that most of our coverage universe now trades at a discount.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Current software valuations are well-below the zero-growth scenario intrinsic value, implying dislocation even with modest growth.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Furthermore, with our current coverage trading at valuation levels well-below our zero-growth scenario, we’d note that should even modest growth be accounted for, our coverage would still screen dislocated relative to its intrinsic value.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Datadog is a high-quality asset with an attractive combination of 26% top-line growth and 29% FCFM as of FY24.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Datadog (12-month PT $127): We highlight Datadog in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with an attractive combination of 26% top-line growth and 29% FCFM as of FY24.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Datadog's strategic positioning as a leading end-to-end observability platform should enable it to sustain a growth rate of 20%+ for the foreseeable future.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We continue to believe that Datadog’s strategic positioning as a leading end-to-end observability platform should enable it to sustain a growth rate of 20%+ for the foreseeable future.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Datadog's increasing contribution from AI revenue (6% of ARR in F4Q24 vs. 3% in F4Q23) is expected to continue outpacing core growth.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our conviction is further supported by the increasing contribution from AI revenue (comprising 6% of ARR in F4Q24 vs. 3% in F4Q23), which we believe can continue outpacing the growth of the ‘Core’.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Datadog shows healthy in-quarter metrics with NRR inflecting from mid-110s to high 110s in F4Q.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Coupled with healthy in-quarter metrics (NRR inﬂecting from mid-110’s to high 110’s in F4Q), we remain constructive on Datadog’s ability to scale into a $10bn business, support by secular tailwinds such as platform consolidation and DX / Gen-AI value garnering at the platform layer near-term as a robust expansion opportunity.

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow is a high-quality asset with 20%+ long-term Subscription Revenue growth potential, a strong and expanding FCF margin profile (>30%), and a solid balance sheet.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: ServiceNow (12-month PT $1,150): We highlight ServiceNow as a high-quality asset with 20%+ long-term Subscription Revenue growth potential, a strong and expanding FCF margin proﬁle (>30%), and a solid balance sheet, meeting the key characteristics of a defensive software asset while still providing growth at-scale.

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow's broadly reiterated FY25 Subscription Revenue guidance of +19.5% CC (albeit -25 bps below prior) is underpinned by strong cRPO growth (+22% CC in F1Q) and healthy pipeline trends.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We see additional support from ServiceNow’s broadly reiterated FY25 Subscription Revenue guidance of +19.5% CC (albeit -25 bps below prior), underpinned by strong cRPO growth (+22% CC in F1Q) and healthy pipeline trends.

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow's current guidance appropriately contemplates risk from ongoing geopolitical uncertainty and potential Federal IT spending rationalization.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We also believe the current guidance appropriately contemplates risk from ongoing geopolitical uncertainty and potential Federal IT spending rationalization – Management is assuming ~ﬂat Fed NNACV from 2Q-4Q despite +30% PubSec NNACV growth in F1Q.

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow stock is priced at an attractive entry point, being >20% off all-time highs and at ~36x EV/FCF CY26, despite the turbulent macro period.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: With NOW >20% off all-time highs (and at ~36x EV/FCF CY26), we believe the stock is priced at an attractive entry point, notwithstanding the turbulent macro period.

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow's standard for selling on customer value will enable it to continue gaining share of IT budgets against a potentially more constrained spending backdrop.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: ServiceNow remains the standard for selling on customer value, which we believe will enable the company to continue to gain share of IT budgets against a potentially more constrained spending backdrop, as in prior cycles.

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow's deeper foray into the CRM market, rollout of AI-enabled Pro Plus SKUs, and pending Moveworks acquisition will further augment its position.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: This is further complemented by ServiceNow’s deeper foray into the massive CRM market as well as through the rollout of its AI-enabled Pro Plus SKUs, which we believe can be further augmented by the company’s pending Moveworks acquisition.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Monday.com is a high-quality asset with best-in-class unit economics, at a Rule of 60+ profile in FY24.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Monday.com (12-month PT $320): We highlight Monday.com in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with best-in-class unit economics, at a Rule of 60+ proﬁle in FY24, which we believe justiﬁes a premium vs. peers.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Monday.com's path to sustained high 20% growth is supported by newer products scaling (CRM, Dev, Service), bolstered Work Management capabilities driving wider deployments, and an increasing focus on AI products for customer retention and monetization.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We remain constructive on Monday’s path to becoming a strategic software provider and supporting sustained high 20% growth as: 1) Newer products scale (like CRM, Dev, and Service), 2) Bolstered Work Management capabilities drive wider deployments and support expansion upmarket, and 3) An increasing focus on AI products should support customer retention and eventual monetization.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Monday.com shows tangible momentum in AI usage (~10mm total AI actions), launched Monday Expert in March, and introduced consumption-based pricing for AI Blocks.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We highlight Monday’s tangible momentum in AI usage last quarter, with ~10mm total AI actions (vs. ~3mm/~1mm in Q3/Q2), Monday Expert launching in March, and the introduction of consumption-based pricing for AI Blocks (priced per successful request/block).

catalysts.catalyst_details: Snowflake is a high-quality asset with mid-20s topline growth and 25%+ FCFM, supported by stabilizing fundamentals and solidification of long-term growth strategy under current management.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Snowﬂake (12-month PT $205): We highlight Snowﬂake in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with an attractive combination of mid-20s topline growth and 25%+ FCFM, supported by stabilizing fundamentals and solidiﬁcation of long-term growth strategy under the current management.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Snowflake's core Data Warehousing/Engineering opportunity remains intact with NRR steadying at +126%, coupled with accelerating product velocity across Snowpark, Iceberg, and Cortex AI.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We highlight that the core Data Warehousing/Engineering opportunity remains intact with NRR steadying at +126% (vs. +127% in 2Q/3Q), coupled with product velocity accelerating across Snowpark, Iceberg, and Cortex AI.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Snowflake is expected to deliver OpM expansion following an AI investment cycle in CY25, coupled with a more efficient GTM motion, offering FCF support going forward.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: At the same time, we also see Snowﬂake delivering OpM expansion following an AI investment cycle in CY25 coupled with a more efﬁcient GTM motion, offering FCF support going forward.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Snowflake can become core to the development of AI applications at the platform layer and a long-term beneficiary of the Gen-AI cycle as spend permeates from Infrastructure into the Platform layer.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Looking ahead, we believe that as Snowﬂake can become core to the development of AI applications at the platform layer and a long-term beneﬁciary of the Gen-AI cycle as spend permeates from Infrastructure into the Platform layer.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Atlassian is a high-quality asset with durable 20%+ revenue growth and high-20s/low-30% FCF margin through FY27.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Atlassian (12-month PT $305): We highlight Atlassian in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with durable 20%+ revenue growth and high-20s/low-30% FCF margin through FY27 .

catalysts.catalyst_details: Atlassian is expected to execute on its 20% Cloud CAGR through FY27 and grow to become a $10bn business long-term.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As Atlassian’s GTM business evolves and AI services continue to scale, we expect conviction to grow around Atlassian executing on its 20% Cloud CAGR through FY27 and, long term, for Atlassian to grow to become a $10bn business.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Atlassian has over 1 million monthly users of AI capabilities, growing customer enthusiasm, and competitive pricing, supporting its long-term positioning despite less near-term AI revenue upside.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In particular, with >1mn monthly users of AI capabilities and growing customer enthusiasm around adoption, the company’s competitive pricing leaves us increasingly positive around Atlassian’s long-term positioning, despite less near-term revenue upside from AI contribution.

catalysts.catalyst_details: GitLab is a best-in-class compounding growth asset that can continue to drive mid-20s growth and expanding FCF margins amid broader macro uncertainty.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: GitLab (12-month PT $63): We highlight GitLab as a best-in-class compounding growth asset that can continue to drive mid-20s growth and expanding FCF margins amid broader macro uncertainty.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The developer tool ecosystem is identified as first movers in the AI adoption cycle, with many tools already seeing strong adoption and capturing budget share.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In particular, as we highlighted in our Private Software Company Conference note, we see the developer tool ecosystem as ﬁrst movers in the AI adoption cycle, noting many tools are already seeing strong adoption and capturing budget share.

catalysts.catalyst_details: GitLab's strong competitive positioning, best-of-breed Gen-AI products, steadfast execution, and upmarket motion position the company well to execute on its growth opportunity.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe Gitlab’s strong competitive positioning, best-of-breed Gen-AI products, steadfast execution, and upmarket motion position the company well to execute on this opportunity.

catalysts.catalyst_details: GitLab's refined focus on adding new paying customers (mid-market/enterprise), customer expansion, and pace of innovation provides multiple opportunities for long-term growth.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: More broadly, with GitLab’s focus reﬁned around 1) Adding new paying customers (particularly in the mid-market/enterprise), 2) Customer expansion, and 3) Pace of innovation, we believe there remain multiple opportunities to drive long-term growth.

catalysts.catalyst_details: GitLab is emerging as a clear leader in the fragmented DevOps market, driven by its full suite, ease-of-use, scalability, and focus on R&D and innovation, which can drive expanding wallet-share in a $40bn+ opportunity.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: GitLab is emerging as a clear leader in the fragmented DevOps market driven by its full suite covering the stages in the entire SDLC market, ease-of-use, scalability and focus on R&D and innovation, all of which can drive expanding wallet-share in a $40bn+ opportunity.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Samsara is a best-in-class compounding growth asset that can continue to deliver 30%+ topline growth amid broader macro uncertainty.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Samsara (12-month PT $46): We highlight Samsara as a best-in-class compounding growth asset that can continue to deliver 30%+ topline growth amid broader macro uncertainty.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Samsara possesses significant competitive advantages in a large, underpenetrated TAM, while delivering consistent product innovation with operating leverage.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our outlook is underpinned by Samsara’s signiﬁcant competitive advantages in a large, underpenetrated TAM while delivering consistent product innovation with operating leverage.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Samsara shows expanding DBNRR across core (>115%) and large customers (>120%), growing contribution from emerging products (15% of NNACV), accelerating international growth (17% NNACV), and strength across key verticals (e.g., Construction).
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We are further encouraged by Samsara’s expanding DBNRR across the core at >115% and large customers at >120%, growing contribution from emerging products (contributing 15% of NNACV), accelerating international growth (contributing 17% NNACV), and strength across key verticals (e.g., Construction).

catalysts.catalyst_details: Samsara is evolving into a broader workflow application platform beyond its core telematics and driver safety products, expanding its footprint across a significantly larger asset management market.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Over time, we see Samsara evolving into a broader workﬂow application platform beyond its core telematics and driver safety products, expanding its footprint across a signiﬁcantly larger asset management market outside of core domains.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Rubrik is a best-in-class growth asset that can drive 20%+ ARR growth long-term with an elevated FCF margin expansion cadence.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Rubrik (12-month PT $82): We highlight Rubrik in the current macro environment as a best-in-class growth asset that can drive 20%+ ARR growth long-term with an elevated FCF margin expansion cadence.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Rubrik is uniquely suited to disrupt legacy competitors in the $11B+ Backup and Recovery market with its next-gen data security platform and expanding functionality.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe Rubrik is uniquely suited to continue to disrupt legacy competitors in the $11B+ Backup and Recovery market given its next-gen data security platform with ever-expanding functionality.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Rubrik's ongoing innovation velocity (extending workload coverage, launching new products like Identity Recovery, integrating DSPM within RSC) reaffirms its ability to expand its Cyber Resilience TAM.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We remain compelled by Rubrik’s ongoing innovation velocity - extending workload coverage (re: Postgres, Red Hat OpenShift, OCI), launching new products such as Identity Recovery and natively integrating DSPM within RSC – reafﬁrming our view that Rubrik can expand its Cyber Resilience TAM.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Rubrik is well-positioned to execute due to its strong value proposition to enterprises, enabling CIOs to repurpose existing backup budgets for a next-generation data security platform with considerable TCO reduction.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Even in a constrained macro environment, we see Rubrik well-positioned to execute given the company’s already strong value proposition to the enterprise that enables CIOs to repurpose existing backup budgets for a next-generation data security platform that often comes with considerable TCO reduction vs. incumbent offerings.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: High-quality asset with strong long-term growth potential, expanding FCF margins, solid balance sheet, attractive valuation, ability to gain IT budget share, and strategic expansion into CRM and AI-enabled products.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We highlight ServiceNow as a high-quality asset with 20%+ long-term Subscription Revenue growth potential, a strong and expanding FCF margin proﬁle (>30%), and a solid balance sheet, meeting the key characteristics of a defensive software asset while still providing growth at-scale. We see additional support from ServiceNow’s broadly reiterated FY25 Subscription Revenue guidance of +19.5% CC (albeit -25 bps below prior), underpinned by strong cRPO growth (+22% CC in F1Q) and healthy pipeline trends. We also believe the current guidance appropriately contemplates risk from ongoing geopolitical uncertainty and potential Federal IT spending rationalization – Management is assuming ~ﬂat Fed NNACV from 2Q-4Q despite +30% PubSec NNACV growth in F1Q. With NOW >20% off all-time highs (and at ~36x EV/FCF CY26), we believe the stock is priced at an attractive entry point, notwithstanding the turbulent macro period. ServiceNow remains the standard for selling on customer value, which we believe will enable the company to continue to gain share of IT budgets against a potentially more constrained spending backdrop, as in prior cycles. This is further complemented by ServiceNow’s deeper foray into the massive CRM market as well as through the rollout of its AI-enabled Pro Plus SKUs, which we believe can be further augmented by the company’s pending Moveworks acquisition.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: High-quality asset with best-in-class unit economics, strong product foundation, scaling go-to-market initiatives, durable margin profile, and sustained high growth driven by new products and increasing focus on AI.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We highlight Monday.com in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with best-in-class unit economics, at a Rule of 60+ proﬁle in FY24, which we believe justiﬁes a premium vs. peers. We enter FY25 positive on Monday’s strong product foundation, scaling go-to-market initiatives, and durable margin proﬁle. We remain constructive on Monday’s path to becoming a strategic software provider and supporting sustained high 20% growth as: 1) Newer products scale (like CRM, Dev, and Service), 2) Bolstered Work Management capabilities drive wider deployments and support expansion upmarket, and 3) An increasing focus on AI products should support customer retention and eventual monetization. We highlight Monday’s tangible momentum in AI usage last quarter, with ~10mm total AI actions (vs. ~3mm/~1mm in Q3/Q2), Monday Expert launching in March, and the introduction of consumption-based pricing for AI Blocks (priced per successful request/block).

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: High-quality asset with durable revenue growth and strong FCF margins, evolving GTM business, scaling AI services, and competitive AI pricing supporting long-term positioning and growth.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We highlight Atlassian in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with durable 20%+ revenue growth and high-20s/low-30% FCF margin through FY27. As Atlassian’s GTM business evolves and AI services continue to scale, we expect conviction to grow around Atlassian executing on its 20% Cloud CAGR through FY27 and, long term, for Atlassian to grow to become a $10bn business. In particular, with >1mn monthly users of AI capabilities and growing customer enthusiasm around adoption, the company’s competitive pricing leaves us increasingly positive around Atlassian’s long-term positioning, despite less near-term revenue upside from AI contribution. With customers expressing slight hesitation in buying behavior (though not a pause/freeze) given macro uncertainty, this strategy may prove strategically sound longer-term, supporting near-term experimentation with Rovo that can further establish Atlassian over time.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: High-quality asset with attractive mid-20s topline growth and strong FCFM, supported by stabilizing fundamentals, new product growth optionality, efficient GTM motion, and long-term benefits from the Gen-AI cycle.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We highlight Snowﬂake in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with an attractive combination of mid-20s topline growth and 25%+ FCFM, supported by stabilizing fundamentals and solidiﬁcation of long-term growth strategy under the current management. We believe Snowﬂake is on a ﬁrmer footing to re-establish itself as a compounding growth asset with FCF support, underpinned by stabilizing core business, growth optionality via new products, and a more efﬁcient GTM motion. We highlight that the core Data Warehousing/Engineering opportunity remains intact with NRR steadying at +126% (vs. +127% in 2Q/3Q), coupled with product velocity accelerating across Snowpark, Iceberg, and Cortex AI. At the same time, we also see Snowﬂake delivering OpM expansion following an AI investment cycle in CY25 coupled with a more efﬁcient GTM motion, offering FCF support going forward. Looking ahead, we believe that as Snowﬂake can become core to the development of AI applications at the platform layer and a long-term beneﬁciary of the Gen-AI cycle as spend permeates from Infrastructure into the Platform layer.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: High-quality asset with strong top-line growth and FCFM, strategic positioning as a leading observability platform, resilience to macro uncertainty, increasing AI revenue contribution, and potential to scale into a $10bn business.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We highlight Datadog in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with an attractive combination of 26% top-line growth and 29% FCFM as of FY24. We continue to believe that Datadog’s strategic positioning as a leading end-to-end observability platform should enable it to sustain a growth rate of 20%+ for the foreseeable future. Despite near-term macro uncertainty, we believe that Datadog will remain resilient and continue to deliver strong execution, given the mission-critical use-cases supported by its observability and monitoring solutions. Our conviction is further supported by the increasing contribution from AI revenue (comprising 6% of ARR in F4Q24 vs. 3% in F4Q23), which we believe can continue outpacing the growth of the ‘Core’. Coupled with healthy in-quarter metrics (NRR inﬂecting from mid-110’s to high 110’s in F4Q), we remain constructive on Datadog’s ability to scale into a $10bn business, support by secular tailwinds such as platform consolidation and DX / Gen-AI value garnering at the platform layer near-term as a robust expansion opportunity.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Best-in-class compounding growth asset with mid-20s growth and expanding FCF margins, strong competitive positioning in the developer tool ecosystem, best-of-breed Gen-AI products, and a comprehensive suite covering the entire SDLC market.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We highlight GitLab as a best-in-class compounding growth asset that can continue to drive mid-20s growth and expanding FCF margins amid broader macro uncertainty. In particular, as we highlighted in our Private Software Company Conference note, we see the developer tool ecosystem as ﬁrst movers in the AI adoption cycle, noting many tools are already seeing strong adoption and capturing budget share. We believe Gitlab’s strong competitive positioning, best-of-breed Gen-AI products, steadfast execution, and upmarket motion position the company well to execute on this opportunity. More broadly, with GitLab’s focus reﬁned around 1) Adding new paying customers (particularly in the mid-market/enterprise), 2) Customer expansion, and 3) Pace of innovation, we believe there remain multiple opportunities to drive long-term growth. GitLab is emerging as a clear leader in the fragmented DevOps market driven by its full suite covering the stages in the entire SDLC market, ease-of-use, scalability and focus on R&D and innovation, all of which can drive expanding wallet-share in a $40bn+ opportunity.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Best-in-class compounding growth asset with 30%+ topline growth, significant competitive advantages in a large, underpenetrated TAM, consistent product innovation, expanding DBNRR, and growing contribution from emerging products and international markets.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We highlight Samsara as a best-in-class compounding growth asset that can continue to deliver 30%+ topline growth amid broader macro uncertainty. Our outlook is underpinned by Samsara’s signiﬁcant competitive advantages in a large, underpenetrated TAM while delivering consistent product innovation with operating leverage. We are further encouraged by Samsara’s expanding DBNRR across the core at >115% and large customers at >120%, growing contribution from emerging products (contributing 15% of NNACV), accelerating international growth (contributing 17% NNACV), and strength across key verticals (e.g., Construction). Over time, we see Samsara evolving into a broader workﬂow application platform beyond its core telematics and driver safety products, expanding its footprint across a signiﬁcantly larger asset management market outside of core domains. With a compounding growth trajectory within a largely underpenetrated TAM, we remain constructive on the potential for Samsara to eventually break into the $5bn scale over the long-term.

bullish_views.bullish_debate_point: Best-in-class growth asset with 20%+ ARR growth and elevated FCF margin expansion, uniquely positioned to disrupt the Backup and Recovery market with its next-gen data security platform, ongoing innovation, and strong value proposition for TCO reduction.
bullish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We highlight Rubrik in the current macro environment as a best-in-class growth asset that can drive 20%+ ARR growth long-term with an elevated FCF margin expansion cadence. We believe Rubrik is uniquely suited to continue to disrupt legacy competitors in the $11B+ Backup and Recovery market given its next-gen data security platform with ever-expanding functionality. We remain compelled by Rubrik’s ongoing innovation velocity - extending workload coverage (re: Postgres, Red Hat OpenShift, OCI), launching new products such as Identity Recovery and natively integrating DSPM within RSC – reafﬁrming our view that Rubrik can expand its Cyber Resilience TAM. Even in a constrained macro environment, we see Rubrik well-positioned to execute given the company’s already strong value proposition to the enterprise that enables CIOs to repurpose existing backup budgets for a next-generation data security platform that often comes with considerable TCO reduction vs. incumbent offerings.

thesis_analysis.central_thesis: The software sector is poised for a compelling upside opportunity in CY26, driven by stabilizing fundamentals, robust free cash flow generation capabilities, attractive valuations, and the transformative long-term catalyst of Generative AI, despite near-term macroeconomic and tariff-related uncertainties.

sub_claims.claim: Software fundamentals are stabilizing and showing signs of improvement after a period of deceleration.

evidence_list.content: The worst is behind us for software fundamentals with leading growth indicators (cRPO, NER, new logos) stabilizing in C2H24 after a multi-year deceleration from peak levels.
NER slightly inﬂected to 112% after 10 consecutive quarters of decline and 1 quarter of stability.
QoQ customer growth remained stable at +3% in C2H24.
cRPO growth (YoY) broadly stable through C2H24, outpacing coverage NTM revenue growth expectations.
Companies in our coverage are entering CY25 with a slightly higher proportion of committed revenue vs. NTM guidance relative to the prior years (68% vs. 67% in C1Q24).

sub_claims.claim: Software companies possess strong financial resilience and the ability to preserve Free Cash Flow (FCF) through cost agility.

evidence_list.content: Management teams remain well-equipped to preserve FCF through cost agility, with Gen-AI investments unaffected.
FCF growth can remain resilient if our slowdown scenario transpires.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High credibility - analyst assessment based on sensitivity analysis

evidence_list.content: Software models provide room to adjust variable costs, particularly in S&M, without compromising strategic R&D investments like Gen-AI.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High credibility - analyst assessment of business model

evidence_list.content: The sector is structurally more proﬁtable as companies right-sized their cost structures following a post-COVID growth slowdown (CY22-24).
Software companies today are expected to deliver an average 20% FCFM in the NTM (per Consensus, Exhibit 13).
Variable cost leverage can likely blunt the impact of any potential revenue growth slowdown.
Companies are already running leaner following a multi-year period of workforce optimization and vendor consolidation.
OpM’s are only expected to expand +200 bps across our coverage in CY25, with many companies highlighting additional GTM hiring and investments in R&D for Gen-AI initiatives.

sub_claims.claim: Current valuations for software companies are compelling and present significant upside potential.

evidence_list.content: At ~6x EV/Sales NTM, downside is modest if CY25 estimates are revised lower, while a re-rating to ~8x (normalized multiple) could unlock meaningful upside into CY26.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High credibility - analyst assessment with specific multiples

evidence_list.content: We continue to highlight the disconnect between current valuations and fundamentals (GSe 46% discount to Intrinsic Value).
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High credibility - analyst assessment with specific discount

evidence_list.content: The sector holistically is better positioned to absorb cuts given the -21%+ de-rating in EV/S (NTM) multiples, which is now near multi-year lows.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High credibility - analyst assessment with specific data

evidence_list.content: Current valuation levels provide a solid entry point for the broader comp group, especially with prospects of a Gen-AI driven recovery in CY26.
Our Intrinsic Value Analysis, which indicates that most of our coverage universe now trades at a discount.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High credibility - analyst assessment based on proprietary model

evidence_list.content: Using a 4% yield, the average revenue multiple for our coverage would be 9.9x. As 9.9x remains higher than what the coverage is valued at currently (6.8x, excluding the stocks excluded in the Intrinsic Value Analysis), we believe there is room for upward revision in multiples.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High credibility - specific model output and comparison

evidence_list.content: A 3% and 5% yield imply intrinsic values of 13.3x and 8.0x (+94% / +17%) from current levels, respectively.

sub_claims.claim: Generative AI (Gen-AI) is a powerful and transformative long-term catalyst for software growth and value creation.

evidence_list.content: We see AI as the sector’s key catalyst, especially as focus moves from infra/training CapEx to inferencing use cases at the platform and application layers.
Gen-AI is poised to drive the next leg of software growth despite short-term tariff-induced volatility.

In a productivity-starved economy, Gen-Al is a compelling lever to offset hiring constraints and cost pressures, making AI readiness and operational agility central to navigating the next leg of the cycle.
Laying the groundwork in fostering AI adoption could translate to growth upside in 2026.
AI tools that drive internal productivity and cost optimization are best poised to see adoption.

Platforms with hybrid consumption models (e.g., ServiceNow Assist, Salesforce’s Agentforce, Zoom AI companion, etc.) are well positioned to scale AI experimentation and roll-out ﬁrst within pockets of an organization.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High credibility - analyst assessment with examples

evidence_list.content: Product innovation cycle in 2025 can pave the way to AI-driven value creation in 2026.

Adobe generating over $125M of Al-speciﬁc ARR and inﬂuencing more than $3.5B of Al-enabled bookings; ServiceNow saw Pro Plus deals more than quadruple yoy, including 39 deals with 3+ Now Assist products; Salesforce counting 3,000 paid AgentForce customers and roughly $900M of combined Data Cloud and Al ARR; Intuit reporting a 20% higher ecosystem attach rate for Al-powered QuickBooks Live users; Microsoft disclosing a $13B annualized run rate from AI services; Datadog attributing 6% of both yoy growth (CY24) and total ARR to Al-native clients; Twilio reported $260M in revenue from customers building in the AI space.
evidence_list.credibility_assessment: High credibility - specific company data points

evidence_list.content: These results conﬁrm that Al is moving from pilot to production, reinforcing our conviction that Al-ready, operationally disciplined franchises will emerge as clear winners once macro volatility subsides.

bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We explore two key themes: 1) Potential risk to current guidance (CY25) and 2) Best positioned software companies. At best, Management teams will hold guidance unchanged near-term, although it’s possible we might enter an air-pocket as the year unfolds as we previously highlighted here.

bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We’ve conducted a sensitivity analysis around net new revenue (NNR), assuming worst-case scenarios of -5% to -20% NNR cuts, though we do not subscribe to this outcome yet. This implies CY25 average revenue growth (GSe +13%) could see a 100–300 bps reduction (albeit -4% drawdown in USD since C1Q close vs. major currencies could be a partial mitigant).

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Recent software multiple compression.

bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: For subscription and consumption models alike, Management teams are likely to call out some level of deal cycle elongation, reduced contract sizes and/or broader freezes in software procurement entering the June quarter (April/May); at a minimum, they’re likely to acknowledge the risk of such a dynamic impacting upcoming quarters.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Downside scenario contemplates a slowdown in key drivers of the software growth algorithm (Net expansion, New logos, Customer retention) relative to Management guidance.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our downside scenario contemplates a slowdown in key drivers of the software growth algorithm relative to Management guidance. To determine the impact, we evaluated the implied Net New Revenue for CY25 based on Management guidance and reduced that assumption anywhere from -5% to -20%, which we see as an appropriate starting point given broad uncertainty as to what shape tariff policy ultimate takes in the comings days, weeks and months. We believe this approach also effectively captures the potential degradation to all or some critical components of the software growth algorithm: 1) Net expansion, 2) New logos and 3) Customer retention.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Average revenue growth for coverage in CY25 could come down -70 bps to -280 bps with NNR cuts.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our net new revenue sensitivity analysis implies that average revenue growth for our coverage in CY25 (GSe: 13%) could come down -70 bps in a more conservative scenario of a -5% cut to NNR and upwards of -280 bps in a downside scenario where NNR is cut by -20% (Exhibit 2).

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Potential margin degradation if software companies are unable to dynamically adapt to a slower demand environment (e.g., if NNR cuts are not met with operating leverage).
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We also provide an illustrative downside scenario analysis where cuts to net new revenue are not met with a commensurate step-up in operating leverage. Assuming a worst-case scenario where CY25 NNR is cut by -10% vs. initial guidance and fully ﬂows through to the FCF line, our coverage would see an average ~100 bps reduction in CY25 FCFM’s and a ~200 bps decline in their ‘Rule of’ proﬁles (Exhibit 3). This downside case assumes no variable cost ﬂexibility and illustrates the potential margin degradation if software companies are unable to dynamically adapt to a slower demand environment.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Potential for modest cuts to net new revenue if trends softened post-tariff announcement.
bearish_views.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our analysis of net new revenue trends pre- and post-guidance points suggests potential for modest cuts if trends softened post-tariff announcement.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: Management teams adjusted to rising interest rates and macroeconomic uncertainty, leading to tightening IT budgets and prioritization of cost optimization over new digital initiatives.

bearish_views.bearish_debate_point: The software sector has been contending with a sluggish macro environment, leading to growth deceleration from +31% in CY22 to +15% in CY24.

bearish_analysis.file_Id: Americas Technology_ Software_ Navigating Turbulence Part VIII_ Mark-to-Market for Potential Tariff Impact.pdf

facts.fact_summary: The report is titled 'Americas Technology: Software Navigating Turbulence Part VIII: Mark-to-Market for Potential Tariff Impact'.
facts.original_sentence: Americas Technology: Software Navigating Turbulence Part VIII: Mark-to-Market for Potential Tariff Impact

facts.fact_summary: The report was published on April 24, 2025, at 4:06 AM PDT.
facts.original_sentence: 24 April 2025 | 4:06AM PDT

facts.fact_summary: Goldman Sachs does and seeks to do business with companies covered in its research reports.
facts.original_sentence: Goldman Sachs does and seeks to do business with companies covered in its research reports.

facts.fact_summary: A tariff announcement occurred on April 2nd.
facts.original_sentence: Following the April 2nd tariff announcement, we are following up on our initial Tariff Note to provide a range of Revenue/FCF scenarios (Exhibit 2) for our broader coverage to help investors better assess the potential knock-on effects to broader software purchasing behavior relative to the operating assumptions implicit in Management guidance exiting C4Q24.

facts.fact_summary: The software sector experienced a material operating leverage cycle in the past few years due to slowing growth, leading most companies to right-size their cost basis through vendor consolidation, hiring pauses, and expense containment.
facts.original_sentence: Our basis centers on the material op. leverage cycle observed in the past few years as software growth slowed considerably, with most companies right-sizing their cost basis through a combination of vendor consolidation, hiring pauses (or RIFs) and broader discretionary/variable expense containment.

facts.fact_summary: In CY22, Net New Revenue (NNR) guidance came in +3% above Consensus.
facts.original_sentence: In CY22, NNR guidance came in +3% above Consensus as many companies were still observing healthy demand signals from the COVID-induced demand pull-forward as digitization trends accelerated.

facts.fact_summary: In both CY23 and CY24, NNR estimates decreased by -7% to -10% when company guidance was issued.
facts.original_sentence: However, in both CY23 and CY24, NNR estimates came down -7% to -10% when company guidance was issued as Management teams adjusted to the new reality of rising interest rates and macroeconomic uncertainty, which led to tightening IT budgets and the relative prioritization of cost optimization over new digital initiatives.

facts.fact_summary: Net new business growth for broader software began to contract sharply in C2H22.
facts.original_sentence: The earliest representation of this dynamic began in C2H22, when net new business growth started to contract sharply for broader software (Exhibit 8).

facts.fact_summary: Revenue growth for the coverage decelerated from +31% in CY22 to +15% in CY24.
facts.original_sentence: The sector has been contending with a sluggish macro ever since, representative in growth for our coverage decelerating from +31% in CY22 to +15% in CY24 (Exhibit 5).

facts.fact_summary: Since the announcement of tariffs, net new revenue estimates for the coverage have decreased by -1.5%, now standing at -7.1% compared to pre-guidance estimates.
facts.original_sentence: We note that since the announcement of tariffs, net new revenue estimates for our coverage have come down -1 .5% (now -7 .1% vs. pre-guidance); this, however, is still well within the historical window Management teams typically guide to (-7-10%).

facts.fact_summary: Leading indicators for revenue growth began to firm up in C2H24, with Net Expansion Rate (NER) stabilizing in the low-110s and sequential customer growth modestly inflecting to +3% (from a +2% trough in C1H24) across the coverage.
facts.original_sentence: Leading indicators for revenue growth were beginning to ﬁrm up in C2H24, with NER stabilizing in the low-110’s (Exhibit 7) and sequential customer growth inﬂecting modestly to +3% (from a +2% trough in C1H24, Exhibit 6) across our coverage.

facts.fact_summary: Companies in the coverage are entering CY25 with 68% of committed revenue versus NTM guidance, a slight increase from 67% in C1Q24.
facts.original_sentence: While the environment has clearly changed, we note that companies in our coverage are entering CY25 with a slightly higher proportion of committed revenue vs. NTM guidance relative to the prior years (68% vs. 67% in C1Q24), which could help put a ceiling on the magnitude of potential negative guidance revisions (if any at all).

facts.fact_summary: In CY22, operating margins (OpM) for the coverage expanded by +200 bps, despite revenue growth slowing from +48% in CY21 to +31% in CY22.
facts.original_sentence: Recall that in CY22, our coverage only expanded OpM’s +200 bps (Exhibit 12) despite revenue growth slowing from +48% (CY21) to +31% (CY22).

facts.fact_summary: In CY23, operating margins (OpM) expanded by +700 bps as management teams optimized for cost takeout.
facts.original_sentence: It wasn’t until the new reality of a higher-for-longer rates environment and more constrained budgetary outlook set in did Management teams begin deliberately optimizing for cost takeout, with CY23 OpM’s expanding +700 bps.

facts.fact_summary: NTM EV/S multiples contracted from approximately 16x to 5x from peak (December 2021) to trough (January 2023).
facts.original_sentence: Reﬂecting back, we note that NTM EV/S multiples contracted from ~16x to ~5x from peak-to-trough (Dec. 21 to Jan. 23) as investors contended with a rising interest rate environment and early signals of a weaker demand backdrop set against still demanding top-line growth expectations, all with very little FCF support (Consensus NTM FCFM of +13% in early-2022).

facts.fact_summary: Software companies are expected to deliver an average 20% Free Cash Flow Margin (FCFM) in the NTM, according to Consensus.
facts.original_sentence: By contrast, software companies today are expected to deliver an average 20% FCFM in the NTM (per Consensus, Exhibit 13).

facts.fact_summary: Software multiples are currently -21%+ off their recent peak of 8.3x, now at approximately 6.6x.
facts.original_sentence: We believe this leaves the sector better positioned to absorb a potential top-line growth slowdown with less pronounced risk of another severe de-rating in valuations, especially with software multiples now -21%+ off their recent peak of 8.3x (now ~6.6x, Exhibit 14).

facts.fact_summary: AI-native application companies have not yet reached the public markets.
facts.original_sentence: AI-native application companies have yet to reach the public markets.

facts.fact_summary: Gen-AI traction at the application layer remains limited outside of DevOps use cases, as observed at a recent PCSIC Conference.

facts.original_sentence: Our coverage is down -15% YTD, underperforming the NASDAQ by ~2 pp., as a combination of macroeconomic headwinds- tariff concerns, public sector austerity, and broader market volatility- has weighed on investor sentiment and raised concerns around weakening enterprise software demand.

facts.fact_summary: Datadog had 26% top-line growth and 29% FCFM as of FY24.
facts.original_sentence: Datadog in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with an attractive combination of 26% top-line growth and 29% FCFM as of FY24.

facts.fact_summary: AI revenue comprised 6% of ARR in F4Q24, up from 3% in F4Q23.
facts.original_sentence: Our conviction is further supported by the increasing contribution from AI revenue (comprising 6% of ARR in F4Q24 vs. 3% in F4Q23), which we believe can continue outpacing the growth of the ‘Core’.

facts.fact_summary: Net Retention Rate (NRR) inflected from mid-110s to high 110s in F4Q.
facts.original_sentence: Coupled with healthy in-quarter metrics (NRR inﬂecting from mid-110’s to high 110’s in F4Q), we remain constructive on Datadog’s ability to scale into a $10bn business, support by secular tailwinds such as platform consolidation and DX / Gen-AI value garnering at the platform layer near-term as a robust expansion opportunity.

facts.fact_summary: ServiceNow has a strong and expanding FCF margin profile of over 30%.
facts.original_sentence: ServiceNow as a high-quality asset with 20%+ long-term Subscription Revenue growth potential, a strong and expanding FCF margin proﬁle (>30%), and a solid balance sheet, meeting the key characteristics of a defensive software asset while still providing growth at-scale.

facts.fact_summary: ServiceNow's FY25 Subscription Revenue guidance is +19.5% CC, which is -25 bps below prior guidance, underpinned by strong cRPO growth of +22% CC in F1Q.
facts.original_sentence: We see additional support from ServiceNow’s broadly reiterated FY25 Subscription Revenue guidance of +19.5% CC (albeit -25 bps below prior), underpinned by strong cRPO growth (+22% CC in F1Q) and healthy pipeline trends.

facts.fact_summary: ServiceNow Management is assuming approximately flat Federal Net New Annual Contract Value (NNACV) from 2Q-4Q, despite +30% Public Sector NNACV growth in F1Q.
facts.original_sentence: Management is assuming ~ﬂat Fed NNACV from 2Q-4Q despite +30% PubSec NNACV growth in F1Q.

facts.fact_summary: ServiceNow (NOW) is more than 20% off all-time highs and trades at approximately 36x EV/FCF CY26.
facts.original_sentence: With NOW >20% off all-time highs (and at ~36x EV/FCF CY26), we believe the stock is priced at an attractive entry point, notwithstanding the turbulent macro period.

facts.fact_summary: Monday.com had a Rule of 60+ profile in FY24.
facts.original_sentence: Monday.com in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with best-in-class unit economics, at a Rule of 60+ proﬁle in FY24, which we believe justiﬁes a premium vs. peers.

facts.fact_summary: Monday.com saw approximately 10 million total AI actions last quarter (compared to ~3 million in Q3 and ~1 million in Q2), launched Monday Expert in March, and introduced consumption-based pricing for AI Blocks.
facts.original_sentence: We highlight Monday’s tangible momentum in AI usage last quarter, with ~10mm total AI actions (vs. ~3mm/~1mm in Q3/Q2), Monday Expert launching in March, and the introduction of consumption-based pricing for AI Blocks (priced per successful request/block).

facts.fact_summary: Snowﬂake has mid-20s topline growth and 25%+ FCFM.
facts.original_sentence: Snowﬂake in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with an attractive combination of mid-20s topline growth and 25%+ FCFM, supported by stabilizing fundamentals and solidiﬁcation of long-term growth strategy under the current management.

facts.fact_summary: Snowﬂake's Net Retention Rate (NRR) is steadying at +126% (compared to +127% in 2Q/3Q).
facts.original_sentence: We highlight that the core Data Warehousing/Engineering opportunity remains intact with NRR steadying at +126% (vs. +127% in 2Q/3Q), coupled with product velocity accelerating across Snowpark, Iceberg, and Cortex AI.

facts.fact_summary: Atlassian has durable 20%+ revenue growth and high-20s/low-30% FCF margin.
facts.original_sentence: Atlassian in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with durable 20%+ revenue growth and high-20s/low-30% FCF margin through FY27 .

facts.fact_summary: Atlassian has over 1 million monthly users of AI capabilities.
